repo_notes.author: Devin
repo_notes.content: Neurotoxic is a frontend-first single-page application built with React 19 + Vite. Use pnpm for installs and scripts (see package.json). Node >=22 is required. Core gameplay and UI live under src/; server/serverless endpoints (leaderboard) are under api/; server helpers live in lib/. Treat large binary assets in src/assets as referenced data, not primary docs.

repo_notes.author: Devin
repo_notes.content: Priority areas to document: 1) Application architecture and entry points (src/main.tsx, src/App.tsx), 2) State management and reducer/action conventions (src/context), 3) Gameplay orchestration hooks in src/hooks (useTravelLogic, useArrivalLogic, rhythm/minigame hooks), 4) Audio engine & song metadata (src/utils/audio, src/assets), 5) Data/event pools (src/data), and 6) Tests & CI (tests/, e2e/, scripts/, .github/workflows).

repo_notes.author: Devin
repo_notes.content: src/ structure notes: - src/context: reducers, action creators, GameState and state contracts. - src/hooks: orchestration and minigame logic (useTravelLogic, useArrivalLogic, rhythm game hooks). - src/components: UI primitives and minigame UI. - src/scenes: scene components and SceneRouter lazy-loading. - src/utils: pure utilities (gameStateUtils, audio helpers) — document these domain contracts.

repo_notes.author: Devin
repo_notes.content: Testing & quality: Local quality gate is `pnpm run test:all`. Fast developer loop uses `pnpm run test:fast`. Vitest (logic + UI) and node test suites split is handled by scripts in package.json and the scripts/ helpers. E2E tests live under e2e/ and use Playwright. Document test harness entrypoints and custom test utilities under tests/.

repo_notes.author: Devin
repo_notes.content: I18n guidance: Locales live in public/locales/en/ and public/locales/de/. All user-facing text is localized via `t('...')` or `<Trans>`. When adding or changing keys, update both locale files and run `pnpm run i18n:extract`. Document namespace conventions (ui:, scenes:, events:) rather than dumping raw translation files.

repo_notes.author: Devin
repo_notes.content: Audio & songs: Audio uses Tone.js and a dedicated audio engine; the canonical clock is `audioEngine.getGigTimeMs()`. Song metadata is in src/data/songs.ts; large binary assets (MIDI/OGG) live in src/assets. Track-end detection uses setlistCompleted + isNearTrackEnd semantics — document these patterns, not binary contents.

repo_notes.author: Devin
repo_notes.content: APIs and server code: Serverless endpoints live under api/ (example: api/leaderboard). Server helper utilities live in lib/ (e.g., lib/redis.js). If you want explicit API pages, document request/response shapes and error cases for these endpoints.

repo_notes.author: Devin
repo_notes.content: Conventions and TypeScript: The project enforces strict TS/CheckJS rules. Avoid `any`; prefer `unknown` and narrow at boundaries. Reducers must be covered by action creators and clamp state in the action creator layer. Consult AGENTS.md files in the repo for many repository-specific conventions and restrictions before generating detailed pages.

repo_notes.author: Devin
repo_notes.content: What to ignore or deprioritize: - node_modules/, dist/, output/, and generated screenshots. - Large binary blobs in src/assets (MIDI/OGG) — reference them, do not include their contents. - Test snapshots and ephemeral developer tooling files.

repo_notes.author: Devin
repo_notes.content: Pages vs repo_notes recommendation: Start with `repo_notes` only (this file) so DeepWiki can auto-plan structured pages. If you require precise titles/hierarchy, switch to an explicit `pages` array and include the COMPLETE set of desired pages (pages is all-or-nothing). After regenerating, iterate with page_notes for surgical guidance.

pages.title: Application Overview
pages.purpose: High-level architecture of the React + Vite single-page application. Describe bootstrap flow (src/main.tsx), App routing (src/App.tsx), SceneRouter responsibilities, and how major subsystems (state, audio, hooks) connect.

pages.title: Entry Points
pages.purpose: Bootstrap and environment: src/main.tsx, index.html, Vite config, environment variables, and dev vs production differences. Explain how the app is mounted and how to start the dev server.

pages.title: Routing & Scenes
pages.purpose: SceneRouter and lazy-loading conventions for scenes under src/scenes. Document default-export expectations, scene lifecycle hooks, and how to add or replace a scene module.

pages.title: Components & UI
pages.purpose: Organization of UI code: src/components and src/ui. Component composition patterns, layout primitives, and styling conventions (Tailwind v4 + CSS variables).

pages.title: Component Library
pages.purpose: Reusable UI components, prop contracts, Storybook patterns (if any), and where to place shared primitives vs feature components. Reference src/components and src/ui for examples.

pages.title: State Management
pages.purpose: State shape, GameState creation, reducer patterns, persistence, and where selectors/actions live (src/context). Include how GameState is hydrated and saved to localStorage.

pages.title: Reducers & Action Creators
pages.purpose: Reducer implementation patterns, adding new actions, actionTypes, and actionCreators. Explain clamping of bounded state and the requirement to call assertNever in reducer default branches.

page_notes.content: When adding an action: update actionTypes, create an action creator that clamps inputs using helpers in src/utils/gameStateUtils.ts, and add a reducer case. Do not use 'any' — prefer 'unknown' and narrow at boundaries. Reducers must call assertNever(action) in the default branch so missing handlers fail typecheck.

pages.title: Hooks & Orchestration
pages.purpose: Orchestration hooks in src/hooks (useTravelLogic, useArrivalLogic, rhythm/minigame hooks). Describe responsibilities, typical patterns for sequencing gameplay, and how hooks dispatch actions into GameState.

pages.title: Gameplay Data & Event Pools
pages.purpose: Data-driven game configuration in src/data — event pools, special events, and how to author new event entries. Explain how event conditions and rewards map to game state changes.

pages.title: Minigames & Rhythm Engine
pages.purpose: Minigame implementations and rhythm engine hooks (src/components/minigames, src/hooks/minigames). Document scoring, timing, and how to add a new minigame and integrate it with scenes and state.

pages.title: Audio Engine
pages.purpose: Audio architecture and integration with Tone.js: audioEngine responsibilities, scheduling patterns, and the canonical clock used for game timing. Describe how to play tracks, schedule callbacks, and handle track-end semantics.

page_notes.content: Audio rules: always use audioEngine.getGigTimeMs() as the canonical clock; do not read Tone.js time directly. Use setlistCompleted + isNearTrackEnd for track-end detection. Song tails are capped to maxNoteTime + NOTE_TAIL_MS; document where NOTE_TAIL_MS is exported. Avoid direct Tone.js scheduling in components — encapsulate in the audio engine.

pages.title: Songs & Assets
pages.purpose: Song metadata in src/data/songs.ts, placement of MIDI/OGG files in src/assets, and rules for adding new tracks (naming, max note times, and asset sizing).

pages.title: Testing & CI
pages.purpose: Test structure, helper scripts, and CI workflows. Document test entry points (scripts/run-*.mjs), vitest splits (logic vs UI), and the repo's quality gate commands.

page_notes.content: Local quality gate: `pnpm run test:all`. Fast loop: `pnpm run test:fast`. Vitest UI and node logic suites are run via scripts in package.json. E2E tests use Playwright under e2e/. For i18n extraction run `pnpm run i18n:extract`. Document test utilities inside tests/ and how to run single test files.

pages.title: E2E & Playwright
pages.purpose: End-to-end test patterns and Playwright setup under e2e/. Describe visual snapshot approach, common helpers in e2e/helpers.js, and CI sharding configuration.

pages.title: Build, Tooling & Scripts
pages.purpose: Build and developer tooling: pnpm scripts, Vite config, TypeScript tasks, and useful scripts/ helpers (simulate, bench, typecheck). Explain developer workflows for common tasks.

pages.title: Localization & i18n
pages.purpose: Locales storage and extraction: public/locales/en and de, usage of `t()` and `<Trans>`, and the extraction process with i18next-parser. Document namespace conventions and where to add translation keys.

pages.title: APIs & Server Code
pages.purpose: Serverless endpoints under api/ (leaderboard), server helpers in lib/, request/response shapes, and usage notes for server-side utilities such as lib/redis.js.

pages.title: TypeScript & Conventions
pages.purpose: Repository TypeScript and CheckJS rules, AGENTS.md conventions, patterns to avoid (no any, prefer unknown), and reducer/action guardrails. Reference jsconfig.checkjs.json and CI typecheck scripts.

pages.title: Performance & Profiling
pages.purpose: Performance hotspots, profiling tips, render/perf checks, and where to run benchmarking tasks (tests/performance, scripts/benchmarks). Note common pitfalls with Pixi.js and audio scheduling.

pages.title: Contributing & PR Workflow
pages.purpose: Developer workflow: conventional commits requirement, lint-staged, husky hooks, PR checks, and how to run the one-command quality gate before opening a PR. Include guidance on AGENTS.md usage and testing expectations.
